segments.paradiso-12-g124: Ad + infinitive (Ad inveggiar) introduces purpose or result ('to [do]'). Inveggiar is an archaic infinitive form; here it governs the following noun phrase cotanto paladino as its object. Mi mosse is passato remoto (third person singular of muovere) with the clitic mi as direct object before the verb; the logical subject (l’infiammata cortesia di fra Tommaso e ’l discreto latino) follows the verb as a postposed subject. L’ is the elided definite article before a vowel (here standing for la), and di + proper names is a genitive/attributive phrase specifying whose courtesy it is; fra is the religious title 'Fra' (brother) attached to Tommaso.
segments.paradiso-12-g125: E is the coordinating conjunction 'and'. Mosse again is passato remoto (3rd sing.) of muovere; here it precedes its subject by poetic inversion and takes meco ('with me') as an adverbial/clitic-like phrase meaning 'with me' rather than a direct object. Questa compagnia is the grammatical subject (this company/group) that performs the motion in this clause.
segments.paradiso-12-g63: Ben is an adverb intensifying parve; parve is the passato remoto of parere meaning “seemed/appeared.” Messo is the past participle used adjectivally here (“placed/positioned” or “well set”); e famigliar di Cristo is a coordinated adjective phrase meaning “and familiar/intimate with Christ,” with di marking the relation to Cristo.
segments.paradiso-12-g64: The initial che introduces a subordinate explanatory clause attached to the previous statement. 'l' is the contracted article (il) before primo; primo amor = “the first love.” The second che introduces a relative clause modifying amor. ’n is an archaic contraction of in (’n = in) and fu is passato remoto of essere (‘was’); manifesto is an adjective meaning “manifest/clear.”
segments.paradiso-12-g65: Fu (passato remoto of essere) is the main verb “was” of this clause; al = a + il (“to/at the”) introduces primo consiglio (“first counsel” or “first piece of advice”). The relative clause che diè Cristo qualifies consiglio: diè is passato remoto of dare (“gave”), with Cristo as the subject.
segments.paradiso-12-g66: Spesse fïate (archaic spelling with diaeresis marking two vowels) = “often, many times.” Fu ... trovato is a passive/periphrastic construction: fu (passato remoto of essere) with the past participle trovato = “was found.” Tacito e desto are adjectives describing his condition (“silent and awake”); in terra = “on earth,” and da la sua nutrice marks the agent (“by his nurse”).
segments.paradiso-12-g67: Come introduces a comparative/simile “as if,” and dicesse is the imperfect subjunctive of dire, used here to report hypothetical or apparent speech (“as if he were saying”). The subjunctive signals that the following is not presented as literal quoted speech but as an impression.
segments.paradiso-12-g68: This is the quoted utterance. Io is the first-person pronoun (vocative within the quotation). Son is the archaic contracted present of essere (sono = “I am”), used here with the past participle venuto to form a perfect sense (“I have come” — literally “I am come”). A marks direction/purpose and questo = “this.”
segments.paradiso-12-g69: Oh is an exclamatory particle. Padre suo is a possessive phrase in the vocative/reference (“his father”); veramente is an adverb “truly/indeed,” and Felice is a proper name (here used as an exclamation praising or naming the father). The syntax is exclamatory, compressing admiration into a short phrase.
segments.paradiso-12-g70: Oh madre sua is an exclamatory vocative (“O his mother”); veramente again means “truly/indeed,” and Giovanna is the mother’s name. The phrase is an exclamation about her, with the name given as a laudatory tag.
segments.paradiso-12-g71: Se is the conditional conjunction “if,” introducing the following qualification; it begins a protasis that will limit or test the exclamation about the mother.
segments.paradiso-12-g72: Interpretata is a feminine past participle agreeing with madre: “interpreted” or “when the name/word is interpreted/explained.” The participle is used absolutely/elliptically here to mean “if, when interpreted.”
segments.paradiso-12-g73: Val is a contracted/poetic form of vale (from valere) meaning “is worth” or “has value.” Come si dice is an impersonal expression “as one says/ as people say” — come = “as,” si is the impersonal/reflexive marker, and dice = present tense of dire (“say”). The whole means “if (she) is worth, as people say.”
segments.paradiso-12-g114: Io (subject pronoun) + son (poetic contraction of sono) is the present indicative 'I am'. The noun phrase la vita di Bonaventura da Bagnoregio is the predicate nominative introduced by the definite article la and linked to Io son; di marks the genitive/relationship ('life of/from Bonaventure of Bagnoregio').
segments.paradiso-12-g115: Che is a relative pronoun referring back to Bonaventure (who). Ne’ is the contraction of in + the and introduces the locative phrase nei grandi offici ('in the great offices'). Sempre is an adverb 'always'. Pospuosi is an archaic/poetic lexical form whose exact modern equivalent is uncertain; syntactically it functions as the verb/predicate of the relative clause governing la sinistra cura ('the left-hand care').
segments.paradiso-12-g116: Two coordinated subjects (Illuminato e Augustin) are linked to the finite verb son (present indicative, 3rd plural) and the locative quici (poetic form of qui 'here') follows the verb. The order (subject + verb + locative) is normal for narrative/poetic listing.
segments.paradiso-12-g117: The clause begins with che (relative 'who') introducing a past narration about those persons. Fuor here is an archaic/poetic form used in a past predicative sense ('were among' / 'were of'), followed by de (of) + primi scalzi poverelli ('the first barefoot poor ones'). The second che introduces another relative clause: nel capestro ('in the halter' — capestro in a figurative/religious sense) + a Dio ('to God') + si (reflexive) + fero (archaic form) amici — here ferro/fero is an archaic verbal form meaning 'became/made themselves' and governs amici as predicate noun.
segments.paradiso-12-g118: Ugo da San Vittore forms a full proper-name noun phrase (name + toponymic/epithet). È is the present indicative 3rd singular of essere; qui is the locative adverb 'here'. Con elli is a prepositional phrase 'with them' attached to the clause.
segments.paradiso-12-g119: A simple coordinated list introduced by e: Pietro Mangiadore e Pietro Spano; both names are presented in apposition as additional members of the group. The coordinate conjunction e links the two proper names.
segments.paradiso-12-g120: Lo qual is the archaic relative 'who/which' (lo functioning as a demonstrative/article plus qual). Giù is an adverb 'down/below' or a poetic marker, and luce is here a verb (from lucere) 'shines' in 3rd singular; in dodici libelli is a prepositional phrase 'in twelve little books' indicating where/through which he 'shines' (i.e., has been made manifest). The clause thus describes the immediately preceding person.
segments.paradiso-12-g121: A series of names (Natàn profeta e ’l metropolitano Crisostomo e Anselmo e quel Donato) followed by a relative clause ch’a la prim’ arte degnò porre mano. Here ch’ = che introduces the relative clause; degnò is the preterite (passato remoto) 3rd singular of degnare 'to deign', followed by the infinitive porre 'to put' (classic verbal construction 'deigned to put/lay') and mano ('hand') combines with porre to form the idiom porre mano 'to set hand to / begin'. A la prim’ arte is the prepositional phrase indicating the object/field to which the action applies.
segments.paradiso-12-g122: Rabano is a proper name functioning as the subject. È is the present indicative 'is' and qui is the locative adverb 'here'. The short clause states presence.
segments.paradiso-12-g123: This line contains a compacted phrase: lucemi is a poetic contraction of luce + mi ('shines to me' or 'shines at my side'), with the verb lucere carrying the enclitic pronoun mi. Dallato is a contracted/dialectal form of dal lato ('on the side' / 'to the side'). The following noun phrase il calavrese abate Giovacchino is the subject/agent, and di spirito profetico dotato is a postnominal participial phrase (dotato = past participle used adjectivally) meaning 'endowed with prophetic spirit'.
segments.paradiso-12-g74: Non (negation) + prepositional phrase 'per lo mondo' marks motive/purpose: literally 'not for the world'. 'lo' is the masculine singular definite article in front of the noun 'mondo' (an older/poetic form instead of 'il').
segments.paradiso-12-g75: 'per cui' is a relative phrase 'for which' (referring back to 'lo mondo'); 'mo' = now. 's’affanna' is the pronominal verb si + affanna, 3rd person singular present indicative meaning 'is distressed/strives' (here 'worries, is anxious'). The phrase 'di retro ad' is a small locution meaning 'from/after behind' = 'after, in pursuit of', followed by the names Ostïense and Taddeo (objects of that following). Note the reflexive contraction s’ for si.
segments.paradiso-12-g76: 'ma' = 'but'. 'per amor de la verace manna' = 'out of love for the true Manna' (purpose/motive); note 'de' = of (older form of 'di'). 'in picciol tempo' = 'in a short time' (picciol = brief). 'gran dottor si feo' is reflexive pas­sive/causative: 'si feo' = 'he made himself / became' a 'gran dottor' (great doctor/theologian/teacher).
segments.paradiso-12-g77: 'tal che' introduces a result clause: 'so that'. 'si mise a' = pronominal past 'he set himself to' (mise = put/set, here 3rd sg. past); 'circüir la vigna' = 'to go around/encircle the vineyard' (circüir here literal 'encircle'). The relative clause 'che tosto imbianca' = 'which soon whitens/turns white' (tosto = quickly/immediately, imbianca = becomes white).
segments.paradiso-12-g78: 'se' introduces a conditional 'if' (the usual conditional 'se' meaning 'if' — note: the provided senses list did not include that sense). '’l' is the contracted definite article 'il'. 'vignaio' here means the vine-tender/vinedresser (a person who tends the vineyard). 'è' is present of 'essere' (is) and 'reo' = 'guilty/wicked'. Together: 'if the vinedresser is guilty.'
segments.paradiso-12-g79: E = 'and'. 'a la sedia' = 'to the seat/see' (sedia here = the ecclesiastical seat/position, not just a chair). 'che fu già benigna' = 'which was formerly/once kind' (fu = past of essere; già = formerly/once). 'più a’ poveri giusti' = 'especially/more so to the poor righteous' (più + a = more toward).
segments.paradiso-12-g80: 'non per lei' = 'not for her' (negating that the motive was the seat itself). 'lei' = 'her' (the seat, treated grammatically as feminine). This short phrase contrasts the seat with the person who occupies it.
segments.paradiso-12-g81: 'ma per colui che siede' = 'but for him who sits [there]'. 'colui' = 'he who' (demonstrative/identifying pronoun); 'che siede' is a relative clause: 'who sits/presides' (siede can mean 'presides' here, i.e. the occupant of the seat).
segments.paradiso-12-g82: 'che traligna' is a short relative clause modifying 'colui': 'who strays/goes astray'. 'traligna' is present tense, meaning 'deviates' or 'has gone astray' (moral/spiritual deviation).
segments.paradiso-12-g83: 'non dispensare' (infinitive with negation) = 'not to grant/dispense'. In an ecclesiastical context 'dispensare' can mean to grant dispensations or to distribute; here it means 'to grant (exemptions/dispensations)'. The numeric formula 'o due o tre per sei' is a terse medieval expression ('two or three for six') describing the specific dispensations/commutations not granted — the 'o' are simple 'or'.
segments.paradiso-12-g84: 'non la fortuna di prima vacante' = 'nor the fortune/revenues of the former vacancy'. 'fortuna' here means the income/revenues (the 'fortune' of the see); 'di prima' = 'of before/previous', 'vacante' = 'vacant' (the period when the see was vacant).
segments.paradiso-12-g85: 'non decimas' = 'nor the tithes' (direct negation: these were not entrusted). 'decimas' = ecclesiastical tithes (the tenth part).
segments.paradiso-12-g86: 'quae sunt pauperum Dei' is a defining relative clause: 'which are (meant) for the poor of God.' 'quae' = which, 'sunt' = are, 'pauperum' = 'of the poor', 'Dei' here is genitive 'of God' (i.e. 'God’s poor').
segments.paradiso-12-g87: 'addimandò' is a past tense verb meaning 'entrusted, assigned' (here: she did not entrust those tithes). Form and tense are past/punctual — the sentence lists what was not entrusted.
segments.paradiso-12-g88: This long phrase contrasts the previous negatives: 'ma contro al mondo errante licenza di combatter' = 'but (she asked) permission to fight against the wandering world'. 'licenza di combatter' = 'leave/permission to fight'; 'per lo seme del qual' = 'for the seed of which' (relative 'qual' = 'which'); 'ti fascian ventiquattro piante' = 'they bind/wrap you with twenty-four plants' — 'fascian' is 3rd-person plural present indicating the action done by 'they' to 'you' (ti). Grammatically, the segment contains a contrastive conjunction 'ma' and a noun phrase 'licenza di combatter' followed by a relative clause introduced by 'del qual'.
segments.paradiso-12-g89: 'Poi' is an adverb meaning 'then' or 'after that', used as a temporal connector beginning this account of action.
segments.paradiso-12-g90: This phrase uses repeated preposition 'con' ('with/by') to mark instruments: 'dottrina' is a noun meaning teaching/doctrine used as an instrument, 'volere' is a substantive here meaning 'will' (not the verb), and 'insieme' ('together/at once') links the two as jointly operative.
segments.paradiso-12-g91: 'Con l'officio appostolico' further specifies the instrument or authority (literally 'with the apostolic office'); 'si mosse' is the passato remoto reflexive of muoversi (literally 'he moved himself', here 'he set himself in motion'). 'Quasi' introduces a simile ('as/like'); 'torrente' is the image; 'ch’ alta vena preme' is a relative clause ('in which/whose high vein presses'), with 'preme' present tense 'presses/urges' describing the torrent's force.
segments.paradiso-12-g92: 'E' ('and') links the second action. 'Ne li sterpi eretici percosse' uses 'ne' (here meaning 'in/among them' or 'therein') plus the plural article 'li' and 'sterpi' (undergrowth/brambles) qualified by 'eretici' (heretical). 'Percosse' is the passato remoto of percuotere/percuotere-like verb meaning 'struck/struck at'; it takes 'l'impeto suo' ('his/its impulse/force') as the thing that struck in those thickets.
segments.paradiso-12-g93: 'Più vivamente' is a comparative adverbial phrase ('more strongly/more vividly'), 'quivi' is an adverb meaning 'there' (pointing back to the place just mentioned). 'Dove le resistenze eran più grosse' is a relative/result clause: 'dove' = 'where', 'le resistenze' = 'the resistances/oppositions', 'eran' is the imperfect 'were', and 'più grosse' is comparative 'greater/thicker', indicating where the action was most intense.
segments.paradiso-12-g94: 'Di lui' marks source or origin ('from him'); 'si fecer poi' is an impersonal/reflexive construction of fare in passato remoto meaning 'were made' or 'came to be made' (the 'si' creates a passive/impersonal sense). 'Diversi rivi' is the plural subject ('several streams'), and 'onde' here functions as a relative/relative adverb meaning 'from which/whence', introducing the clause that follows. 'L'orto catolico si riga' uses 'si riga' as a passive/reflexive present meaning 'is irrigated/watered' (literally 'is furrowed/wet'), with 'orto catolico' = 'the Catholic (i.e. universal) garden.'
segments.paradiso-12-g95: 'Sì che' introduces a result clause ('so that/with the result that'). 'I suoi arbuscelli' is the subject ('its/his little shrubs' — here 'suo' refers back to the garden, so 'its'), 'stan' is present plural of 'stare' used like 'are' or 'stand' to describe condition, and 'più vivi' is a comparative predicate adjective meaning 'more alive/more vigorous.'
segments.paradiso-12-g96: This is a conditional protasis introduced by Se (if). Tal fu l’una rota de la biga is an inverted subject-verb order typical of poetry: tal (such) qualifies l’una rota (one wheel), and fu is the simple past of essere (was). In che introduces a relative clause ('in which') modifying biga; si difese is reflexive (the Church defended itself) with difese as past indicative; e vinse continues with a second past verb (won). In campo is an adverbial phrase ('on the field' or 'in battle'), and la sua civil briga is the object ('its civic quarrel/strife').
segments.paradiso-12-g97: Ben ti dovrebbe assai esser palese is the main clause: dovrebbe is the conditional of dovere ('ought/should'), with ti as the indirect object ('to you'); esser palese is an infinitive phrase ('to be plain/manifest') functioning as the complementary predicate. Ben and assai intensify the obligation/expectation. L’eccellenza de l’altra is the logical subject/thing expected to be plain: 'the excellence of the other (wheel)'.
segments.paradiso-12-g98: Di cui begins a relative phrase specifying the 'other' just mentioned: di cui = 'of whom/of which' or 'concerning whom'. Tomma is a proper name (Tommaso) and appears as subject; dinanzi al mio venir is a temporal/locative phrase meaning 'before my coming' (dinanzi = before/in front of, here 'prior to'). Fu is the past tense of essere ('was') and modifies cortese ('so courteous'), with sì intensifying cortese.
segments.paradiso-12-g99: Ma introduces contrast ('but'). L’orbita is the subject ('the orbit/path/circuit'); che Fé la parte somma di sua circunferenza is a relative clause: fé is an archaic poetic past (fecè / fece) meaning 'made' or 'constituted', and la parte somma di sua circunferenza specifies 'the highest/outermost part of its circumference.' Word order places the relative clause after orbita for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-12-g100: È derelitta is a simple present statement: è (is) + derelitta (past participle/adjective 'abandoned' used predicatively). The adjective expresses a state resulting from prior action; the comma links this clause to the consequence that follows.
segments.paradiso-12-g101: Sì ch’ introduces a consequence ('so that/with the result that'): ch’ è la muffa dov’ era la gromma. È la muffa states the result ('it is the mold'), and dov’ era is a reduced relative ('where/wherein there was') with era the imperfect of essere ('used to be/was'). Gromma is a noun whose precise meaning is archaic/rare (a cluster or bunch) and is placed in contrast with muffa.
segments.paradiso-12-g102: La sua famiglia is a simple noun phrase ('his/its family/household'); familia here means the group or lineage being spoken about and functions as the sentence's topic subject.
segments.paradiso-12-g103: Che si mosse dritta coi piedi a le sue orme is a relative clause modifying famiglia. Che = who/which; si mosse is a past reflexive verb (moversi: 'moved/advanced'); dritta 'straight' describes the manner; coi piedi a le sue orme is an idiom literally 'with feet to his/its footprints' meaning they followed exactly in his tracks.
segments.paradiso-12-g104: È tanto volta uses è (is/has become) + tanto volta (literally 'so turned/so much turned'): volta here conveys the idea of having turned/changed direction or orientation a great deal; the construction is idiomatic and compressed in poetry.
segments.paradiso-12-g105: Che quel dinanzi a quel di retro gitta is a result clause introduced by che ('so that'): quel dinanzi = 'the one in front', a quel di retro = 'toward that one behind', and gitta (from gettare/giat(t)are in dialectal/poetic use) functions as a verb 'casts/throws/leans toward'. Word order is compact: subject (quel), adverbial specification (dinanzi), target (a quel di retro), and verb gitta.
segments.paradiso-12-g106: E tosto si vedrà de la ricolta de la mala coltura links the previous image to an outcome: e tosto ('and soon'), si vedrà is a future passive/impersonal 'will be seen' or 'one will see', and de la ricolta de la mala coltura means 'of the harvest from bad cultivation' — a metaphorical harvest produced by poor practice.
segments.paradiso-12-g107: Quando il loglio si lagnerà che l’arca li sia tolta is a subordinate temporal clause introduced by quando (when). Il loglio ('the darnel/weed') is personified as subject; si lagnerà is the future reflexive 'will complain/lament', and che l’arca li sia tolta is a subordinate clause with sia (present subjunctive of essere) expressing the idea 'that the ark be taken from it' or 'that the ark has been taken away' (tolta as past participle 'taken away').
segments.paradiso-12-g108: Ben dico is a short emphatic formula: Ben ('indeed/clearly') prefaces dico ('I say/I mean'). The order places the adverb before the verb for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-12-g109: Chi cercasse a foglio a foglio nostro volume is a relative/conditional nominal clause: chi cercasse (whoever searched — imperfect/subjunctive used poetically to mean 'were to search' or 'searched') a foglio a foglio ('page by page') nostro volume ('our book/volume'). The verb cercasse is in a past subjunctive/imperfect form used here with hypothetical/iterative sense.
segments.paradiso-12-g110: Ancor troveria carta u’ leggerebbe 'I’ mi son quel ch’i’ soglio' is a conditional/future-in-the-past idea: ancor ('still/yet') + troveria (poetic or corrupted form intended as 'troverebbe' or 'troveria' as noun; here the sense intended is 'would find') carta ('paper') u’ ('wherein') leggerebbe ('would read') introduces the quotation. The speech inside quotes uses archaic first-person contractions (I’ for 'io') and soglio in the sense of customary seat/state; the whole phrase reports what would be found on the pages.
segments.paradiso-12-g111: Ma non fia da Casal né d’Acquasparta negates the expectation: ma non fia ('but there will not be/from there will not come') da Casal né d’Acquasparta ('from Casal nor from Acquasparta'). Fia is a poetic/future form related to essere ('will be'), used impersonally here.
segments.paradiso-12-g112: Là onde vegnon tali a la scrittura contains a locative/relative phrase: là onde ('from there/whence') + vegnon (they come) + tali a la scrittura ('such ones to the writing' — i.e., such writers/versifiers to the craft). Onde is used in the older/poetic sense 'whence' and vegnon is an archaic present plural 'they come'.
segments.paradiso-12-g113: Ch’uno la fugge e altro la coarta is a closing relative clause: ch’ = che ('that/so that'); uno (one person/one type) la fugge ('avoids it' — fugge used intransitively/transitively as 'avoids/passes over') and altro (another) la coarta ('restricts/constrains it'). The clause contrasts two different reactions to 'the writing' using simple present verbs for general truth.
segments.paradiso-12-g31: L’ is the elided definite article (il → l’) attached to essercito; essercito (an older spelling of esercito) is the noun 'army/host' and is governed by the following prepositional phrase di Cristo, a simple genitive/attributive 'of Christ'.
segments.paradiso-12-g32: This relative clause begins with che 'which/that'; sì caro costò is a fixed idiom 'cost so dearly' (sì = 'so'); costò is the past tense (passato remoto) of costare 'to cost'; a + rïarmar expresses the object/infinitive governed by costò ('to rearm' — here an archaic/poetic infinitive form rïarmar).
segments.paradiso-12-g33: dietro a la 'nsegna is a prepositional phrase 'behind the banner' (la + nsegna = l’insegna with apostrophe). si movea is the imperfect indicative (movea) with reflexive si and means 'was moving' or 'moved' in a continuous sense; tardo is a predicate adjective 'slow/late'. Word order is typical: locative phrase + verb + adjective.
segments.paradiso-12-g34: Two coordinating adjectives modifying 'the army' (understood from previous phrase): sospeccioso 'suspicious' (causing or showing suspicion) and raro 'sparse/unusual' — a poetically compressed description of the army's demeanor.
segments.paradiso-12-g35: A temporal subordinate introduced by quando 'when' followed by lo ’mperador 'the Emperor' with a relative clause che sempre regna 'who always reigns'. Provide a la milizia is a (poetic) present tense form meaning 'provides (for) the militia' — provide is an older spelling; a la milizia = 'to/for the military force'.
segments.paradiso-12-g36: ch’era in forse is a short clause 'which was in doubt/uncertain' (ch’ = che). era is imperfect of essere 'was'; in forse is an idiom meaning 'in uncertainty' (here describing the milizia as wavering/uncertain).
segments.paradiso-12-g37: per sola grazia is an ablative/causal phrase 'by sole/only grace' (per = 'by/through', sola = 'only', grazia = 'grace/favour'), marking the reason or means by which the Emperor acted.
segments.paradiso-12-g38: A contrastive clause introduced by non per esser degna 'not for being worthy': non negates, per esser is the infinitive construction 'for being', and degna is an adjective 'worthy', explaining that the Emperor acted out of grace, not merit.
segments.paradiso-12-g39: Simple coordinating conjunction e 'and' linking the previous idea to what follows.
segments.paradiso-12-g40: come è detto is a parenthetical 'as is said' or 'as has been said' (come = 'as', è = present indicative 'is', detto = past participle 'said'), pointing the reader back to a previous statement.
segments.paradiso-12-g41: a sua sposa soccorse con due campioni is the main action: a sua sposa 'to his spouse' (dative), soccorse is passato remoto of soccorrere 'came to the aid/helped', con due campioni 'with two champions' — campioni = champions/warriors.
segments.paradiso-12-g42: al cui fare is a noun phrase 'at/to whose doing' where al = a + il 'to the', cui is the relative pronoun 'whose/to whom', and fare is a nominalized infinitive 'action/deed' ('the doing'). It introduces the domain (their actions) that follows.
segments.paradiso-12-g43: al cui dire lo popol disvïato si raccorse: parallel structure to the previous phrase (al cui dire = 'at whose saying/word'); lo popol 'the people' (lo = article used with popol in poetic diction); disvïato (past participle) 'led astray/wayward'; si raccorse is passato remoto reflexive meaning 'rallied/turned back together' — 'the misled people rallied at their deeds and words.'
segments.paradiso-12-g44: In quella parte 'In that part/region' begins the locative clause; ove 'where' introduces a relative clause. surge ad aprire is a poetic present 'rises/begins to open' (surge + ad + infinitive aprire). Zefiro dolce is a descriptive noun phrase 'sweet/gentle Zephyr' and le novelle fronde is the object ('the new leaves'); di che si vede Europa rivestire is a relative clause 'of which Europe is seen to clothe' (si vede + infinitive rivestire = passive/impersonal construction 'is seen to clothe').
segments.paradiso-12-g45: non molto lungi al percuoter de l’onde 'not very far from the beating/striking of the waves' — non molto lungi = 'not very far', al percuoter = 'to/at the striking' (percuoter as a noun/gerundial concept), de l’onde = 'of the waves'; dietro a le quali 'behind which' continues the spatial reference.
segments.paradiso-12-g46: per la lunga foga 'because of/through the long ardour' — per expresses cause, la lunga foga = 'the long passion/impetus'; foga is a poetic word for zeal/impetuosity modifying the following clause.
segments.paradiso-12-g47: lo sol talvolta ad ogne uom si nasconde is a compressed natural-philosophical remark: lo sol 'the sun' (poetic article), talvolta 'sometimes', ad ogne uom 'from every man' (ad here with a person) si nasconde 'hides itself' (present tense). The verb si nasconde is pronominal/reflexive meaning 'conceals itself/disappears from view.'
segments.paradiso-12-g48: siede la fortunata Calaroga 'sits the fortunate Calaroga' — siede is present indicative 'sits/is situated'; la fortunata is a descriptive adjective; Calaroga is a proper name/place. sotto la protezion del grande scudo 'under the protection of the great shield' is a locative prepositional phrase; in che soggiace il leone e soggioga is a relative clause 'in which the lion lies and subdues' (soggiace 'lies under/subject to', soggioga 'subdues/is master of' — poetic verbs in -a form).
segments.paradiso-12-g49: dentro vi nacque l’amoroso drudo 'within it was born the loving drudo' — dentro vi = 'within there'; nacque is passato remoto of nascere 'was born'; l’amoroso drudo is a compound nominal phrase 'the loving/beloved drudo' (drudo = an archaic word for lover/ beloved). de la fede cristiana 'of the Christian faith' identifies the order/character.
segments.paradiso-12-g50: il santo atleta 'the holy champion' — atleta used metaphorically for a champion/warrior in the cause of faith. benigno a’ suoi e a’ nemici crudo is an antithetical description: benign to his own (benigno a’ suoi) and crudo 'harsh/cruel' to enemies; both adjectives qualify the same subject.
segments.paradiso-12-g51: e come fu creata, is a simple temporal/introductory clause 'and when she was created' (fu creata = was created/born), introducing the result clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-12-g52: fu repleta 'she was filled' (passive past), sì la sua mente di viva vertute 'so was her mind with living virtue' — fu repleta is a passive perfect-state description; sì = 'so' indicates degree; di viva vertute = 'with lively/living virtue' (vertute = virtue).
segments.paradiso-12-g53: ne la madre is a locution 'in the mother' (ne = in/within, la madre = the mother) referring back to who/where that virtue was manifest.
segments.paradiso-12-g54: lei fece profeta 'she made her a prophet' (lei = 'her' as direct object or subject depending on reading; fece = passato remoto of fare 'made', profeta = 'prophet'). Here Dante means the mother's virtue made (produced) a prophet in her.
segments.paradiso-12-g55: Poi che 'after' + subordinate clause: le sponsalizie fuor compiute is a perfect passive idea in inverted/poetic order — fuor (archaic fuori = furono) compiute = 'were completed' referring to the wedding rites; al sacro fonte intra lui e la Fede 'at the sacred font between him and Faith' specifies place and parties of the sacrament.
segments.paradiso-12-g56: u’ (poetic contraction of 'ove' or 'là dov’') si dotar di mutüa salute: si dotar is a pronominal/intransitive reflexive use of dotare 'to endow/bring a dowry' meaning 'they endowed themselves' (dotar = infinitive in archaic spelling), di mutüa salute = 'with mutual salvation/health' (mutüa = mutual).
segments.paradiso-12-g57: la donna che per lui l’assenso diede: relative clause 'the woman who gave her consent for him' — che = 'who', per lui = 'for him' (in his favor), l’assenso diede is passato remoto 'gave the assent/consent'.
segments.paradiso-12-g58: vide nel sonno 'saw in sleep' (vide = passato remoto of vedere), il mirabile frutto 'the marvelous fruit' is the direct object; ch’uscir dovea 'that was to come forth' uses ch’ = che + the infinitive uscire in archaic form uscir with dovea 'was destined/ought to' (imperfect of dovere) and di lui e de le rede 'from him and from his heirs/descendants'.
segments.paradiso-12-g59: e perché fosse qual era in costrutto is a purpose/result clause: e perché 'and so that/so that', fosse is imperfect subjunctive of essere 'might be', qual era 'such as it was' in costrutto (constructed/nature). The subjunctive expresses the intended/desired condition of the thing named.
segments.paradiso-12-g60: quinci si mosse spirito a nomarlo: quinci 'therefore/hence' + si mosse (passato remoto reflexive form of muovere) 'a spirit was moved' (an impersonal supernatural agent), a nomarlo = 'to name it' (nomar-lo, infinitive + clitic), del possessivo di cui era tutto = 'from/of the possessive of which it was wholly/entirely' — the spirit names the thing from the possessive form by which it was characterized.
segments.paradiso-12-g61: Domenico fu detto is a simple nominative statement: Domenico (proper name, here likely Saint Dominic) + fu detto (passive or 'was called') — passato remoto of essere + detto (past participle) yields 'was called'.
segments.paradiso-12-g62: e io ne parlo 'and I speak of him/it' — ne is a clitic meaning 'about/of it/him' (here 'of him'); sì come de l’agricola 'just as of the farmer' is a comparative formula, che Cristo elesse a l’orto suo per aiutarlo 'whom Christ chose for his garden to help him' — elesse is passato remoto 'chose', a l’orto suo = 'to/for his garden', per aiutarlo 'to help him' (purpose/intent).
segments.paradiso-12-g1: Sì tosto come = an adverbial temporal formula 'as soon as' (Sì tosto = 'immediately/soon'); the clause that follows is the temporal subordinate. l’ultima parola is the direct object ('the last word'). la benedetta fiamma is the subject, and per dir is a purpose/infinitive phrase 'to say/utter'; tolse is passato remoto (3rd sg of togliere) used idiomatically 'took away/ceased' (i.e. 'had ceased to utter').
segments.paradiso-12-g2: a rotar cominciò = perfunctory inversion common in poetry: the infinitive rotar ('to turn') precedes the finite verb cominciò (passato remoto, 'began'), so literally 'began to turn'. la santa mola is the subject ('the holy mill/millstone'), with the article placed before the adjective as normal.
segments.paradiso-12-g3: e nel suo giro tutta non si volse = 'and in its turning it did not complete [itself entirely]': giro = 'circuit/turn', tutta here emphasizes completeness ('completely'); non si volse is reflexive passato remoto of volgersi ('did not turn itself/complete its turning'). prima ch’ = 'before that/ere', introducing the following clause. un’altra di cerchio la chiuse = 'another of the circle closed it' (i.e. another ring/turn enclosed it); chiuse is passato remoto of chiudere.
segments.paradiso-12-g4: e moto a moto e canto a canto = an ablative/iterative expression 'motion by motion and canto by canto' (repetition to mark successive increments); colse is passato remoto (from cogliere/colso) meaning 'gathered/seized/collected' — here 'gathered up' the successive motions and songs.
segments.paradiso-12-g5: canto che tanto vince nostre muse = 'a song that so much surpasses our Muses': canto is the noun 'song', che introduces a relative clause, tanto modifies the verb vince (present 'surpasses'), and nostre muse = 'our Muses' (i.e. our poetic inspirations).
segments.paradiso-12-g6: nostre serene in quelle dolci tube = 'our ... serenities/serenes(?) in those sweet trumpets': the phrase is textual/metricly uncertain (serene is a problematic reading), but grammatically nostre modifies serene; in + quelle dolci tube = 'in those sweet trumpets' (tube = trumpets). Note and flag the uncertain lexical reading of 'serene'.
segments.paradiso-12-g7: quanto primo splendor quel ch’e’ refuse = comparative clause 'as much as the first splendour that it poured forth': quanto ... quanto (= 'as much as'); primo splendor = 'first/primary radiance'; quel ch’ e’ refuse is a relative clause where ch’ = 'that', e' here functions as subject-marker ('it') and refuse (from refudere) is passato remoto 'poured/issued' (poetic).
segments.paradiso-12-g8: Come = 'as/just as' introducing a simile. si volgon is reflexive present 3rd plural of volgersi ('they turn/curve themselves'); per tenera nube = 'through a tender/soft cloud' (per expresses medium/means); due archi paralelli e concolori = 'two arches, parallel and of the same colour' completes the image.
segments.paradiso-12-g9: quando = 'when/whenever'; Iunone is the proper name 'Juno'; a sua ancella iube uses iube (Latin infinitive/present form used as a 3rd-person verb 'orders/bids') with a + indirect object 'to her handmaiden'; the clause gives the occasion for the simile.
segments.paradiso-12-g10: nascendo di quel d’entro quel di fori = gerundial phrase 'one arising from that which is within, that from without': nascendo ('arising') governs two elliptic noun-phrases di quel d'entro (literally 'from that of within') and quel di fori ('that of outside'); word-order is compressed/poetic.
segments.paradiso-12-g11: a guisa del parlar di quella vaga = 'in the manner of the speech of that wandering one' (a guisa = 'in the fashion of'); ch’amor consunse = relative 'whom Love consumed' (consunse = passato remoto 'wasted/consumed'); come sol vapori = simile 'as mere vapours' (sol = only), so the description stresses insubstantiality.
segments.paradiso-12-g12: e fanno qui la gente esser presaga = 'and they make the people here to be forewarned/prophetic': fanno + infinitive esser + predicate presaga is a causative construction 'they cause the people here to be foretelling/expectant'.
segments.paradiso-12-g13: per lo patto che Dio con Noè puose = 'by/through the covenant that God established with Noah': per lo patto = 'by reason of the covenant'; puose is passato remoto (from porre) meaning 'placed/established', introducing the Biblical justification.
segments.paradiso-12-g14: del mondo che già mai più non s’allaga = 'concerning the world which already/ever shall never more be flooded': che introduces the relative clause; già mai più non is a negative temporal phrase 'never again' (già and mai combine with non + più), s’allaga is present/passive reflexive meaning 'is flooded/overflows' (future sense here because of the covenant).
segments.paradiso-12-g15: così di quelle sempiterne rose volgiensi circa noi le due ghirlande = 'thus from those everlasting roses the two garlands are turned around us': cosí = 'so/thus'; di quelle sempiterne rose = genitive/partitive 'of those everlasting roses'; volgiensi is reflexive present 3rd pl 'turn themselves' (the garlands act as subject); circa noi = 'around us'; le due ghirlande = 'the two garlands' (logical subject despite postposed position).
segments.paradiso-12-g16: e sì l’estrema a l’intima rispuose = 'and so the outer answered the inner': e sì = 'and thus/so'; l’estrema = 'the outermost/last one'; a l’intima = 'to the innermost'; rispuose is passato remoto (rispondere) 'answered/returned' in the sense of a responsive movement.
segments.paradiso-12-g17: Poi che = 'after/when' introducing a temporal clause; ’l tripudio e l’altra festa grande = two coordinated nouns 'the jubilation and the other great festival' (tripudio and festa are the objects of the temporal clause). The adjective grande modifies festa; the article l’ contracts before a vowel.
segments.paradiso-12-g18: sì del cantare e sì del fiammeggiarsi luce con luce gaudïose e blande = 'so much in singing and so much in flaming/flashings light with light, joyous and mild': the paired sì ... sì emphasizes both sources (song and flame); fiammeggiarsi is a pronominal verb 'to flame/shine'; luce con luce is chiasmic/light-with-light; gaudïose e blande are coordinating adjectives describing the lights.
segments.paradiso-12-g19: insieme a punto e a voler quetarsi = 'together at the same moment and willing to quiet themselves': insieme a punto = 'at the same moment together' (punto = 'moment/point'); a voler quetarsi = 'with a will to quiet themselves' (voler used as a substantive 'willingness' + pronominal quetarsi 'to become quiet').
segments.paradiso-12-g20: pur come li occhi ch’al piacer che i move conviene insieme chiudere e levarsi = 'just as the eyes, at the pleasure that moves them, must together close and lift themselves': pur come = 'just as'; li occhi is the subject; ch’al piacer che i move is a relative clause ('which, at the pleasure that moves them'); conviene is impersonal/implicative 'it is fitting/necessary' followed by the infinitives chiudere e levarsi ('to close and to be lifted/raise themselves').
segments.paradiso-12-g21: del cor de l’una de le luci nove si mosse voce = 'from the heart of one of the nine lights a voice moved/rose': del cor = 'from the heart', de l’una de le luci nove specifies 'one among the nine lights', si mosse voce is an impersonal/poetic phrasing where a voice 'moved/rose' (si mosse + noun).
segments.paradiso-12-g22: che l’ago a la stella parer mi fece in volgermi al suo dove = 'which made me appear like the needle to the star as I turned toward its place': che introduces the relative clause; l’ago a la stella is the simile 'the needle to the star'; parer mi fece is a causative construction 'made me seem'; in volgermi al suo dove = 'in turning myself to its where/place' (volgermi infinitive with reflexive).
segments.paradiso-12-g23: e cominciò = simple narrative connective 'and it began' (cominciò = passato remoto 3rd sg of cominciare). The colon that follows introduces direct speech.
segments.paradiso-12-g24: «L’amor che mi fa bella mi tragge a ragionar de l’altro duca per cui del mio sì ben ci si favella. = direct speech: 'The love that makes me fair/fine draws me to speak of the other leader/duke.' L’amor ... mi fa bella is a relative clause where 'love' is agent; mi tragge a ragionar = 'draws me to speak/reason'; per cui introduces 'by whom/for whom' and the final impersonal ci si favella = 'people speak (of him) there' (si favella impersonal reflexive 'one speaks').
segments.paradiso-12-g25: Degno è che = 'it is fitting/appropriate that' — degno is predicate adjective and che introduces a subordinate clause expressing what is fitting; the structure is impersonal-with-adjective + che.
segments.paradiso-12-g26: dov’ è l’un = 'where/wherever the one is' with dov’ a contraction of dove before a vowel; è is present of essere and l’un = 'the one' (elided form of l’uno).
segments.paradiso-12-g27: l’altro s’induca = 'the other should be led/induced there': s’induca is pronominal (s’) + induca, here a present subjunctive/optative sense inside the subordinate (i.e. 'that the other be brought/led'); the reflexive marks a mediopassive sense 'be led/bring oneself'.
segments.paradiso-12-g28: sì che, = 'so that' (introducing the result clause): sì = 'so/thus', che = 'that'.
segments.paradiso-12-g29: com’ elli ad una militaro = 'just as he [does] to/for one military force/army': com’ (come) = 'as', elli is an archaic/heavy-form pronoun 'he/that one' (subject), ad una militaro = 'to one military [host/force]' (militaro = 'military/military force/warlike' in this older usage). The phrase is compressed/poetic and elli refers back to the previous leader who unites them.
segments.paradiso-12-g30: così la gloria loro insieme luca = 'thus their glory together shines': così = 'thus/so', la gloria loro = 'their glory', insieme = 'together', luca is an archaic/poetic present form meaning 'shines/is bright' (verb).

sentences.paradiso-12-s25: The ardent courtesy of Brother Thomas and the discreet Latin moved me to extol so great a champion; and this company moved with me.
sentences.paradiso-12-s9: He seemed well placed and intimate with Christ: the first love that was manifest in him was for the first counsel that Christ gave.
sentences.paradiso-12-s10: Often he was found on earth by his nurse, silent and awake, as if saying: 'I have come for this.'
sentences.paradiso-12-s12: O his mother, truly Giovanna — if, when interpreted, she is worth what people say!
sentences.paradiso-12-s21: I am the life of Bonaventura of Bagnoregio, who in high offices always ... the left-hand care.
sentences.paradiso-12-s22: Illuminato and Augustine are here, who were among the first barefoot poor ones who, with the halter, made themselves friends to God.
sentences.paradiso-12-s23: Hugo of Saint Victor is here with them, and Peter Mangiadore and Peter Spano, who below shines in twelve little books; Nathan the prophet and the metropolitan Chrysostom and Anselm and that Donato who deigned to set his hand to the first art.
sentences.paradiso-12-s24: Rabanus is here, and to my left the Calabrian abbot Giovacchino, endowed with a prophetic spirit, shines.
sentences.paradiso-12-s13: Not for the world — for which he now worries, following after the one of Ostia and Taddeo — but out of love for the true Manna he became in a short time a great doctor; so that he set himself to go round the vineyard, which soon whitens, if the vinedresser is guilty.
sentences.paradiso-12-s14: And to the chair that had once been especially kind to the righteous poor — not for her sake but for him who sits there, who goes astray — she did not grant dispensations of two or three for six, nor the former vacant revenues, nor the tithes which are for the poor of God did she entrust; but she asked leave to fight against the wandering world for the seed by which they bind you with twenty-four plants.
sentences.paradiso-12-s15: Then, by teaching and by will together, with the apostolic office he set himself in motion like a torrent whose high vein presses; and his force struck into the heretical brambles, more strongly there where the resistances were greater.
sentences.paradiso-12-s16: From him there were then made several streams by which the Catholic garden is watered, so that its little shrubs stand more alive.
sentences.paradiso-12-s17: If one wheel of the chariot was such — in which the Holy Church defended itself and won its civic struggle on the field — the excellence of the other (wheel), of which Thomas was so courteous before my coming, ought certainly to be plain to you.
sentences.paradiso-12-s18: But the orbit that made the highest part of its circumference is abandoned, so that the mold is where the gromma used to be.
sentences.paradiso-12-s19: His family, which moved straight with feet in his footsteps, has turned so much that the one in front casts himself toward the one behind; and soon the harvest of bad cultivation will be seen, when the darnel will complain that the ark has been taken from it.
sentences.paradiso-12-s20: I say this well: whoever searched our volume sheet by sheet would still find paper where he would read 'I am what I usually am'; but there will be none from Casal nor from Acquasparta, from where such writers come to the writing, that one flees it and another restrains it.
sentences.paradiso-12-s5: The army of Christ, which it cost so dearly to re-equip, was moving slowly, suspicious and sparse behind the banner, when the Emperor who always reigns provided for the militia — which was in doubt — by mere grace, not because it was worthy; and, as has been said, he came to the aid of his spouse with two champions, at whose deeds and whose words the wayward people rallied.
sentences.paradiso-12-s6: In that region where sweet Zephyr begins to open the new leaves, whose covering one sees Europe don, not far from the striking of the waves behind which, because of the long ardour, the sun sometimes hides itself from every man, sits the fortunate Calaroga beneath the protection of the great shield in which the lion lies and subdues; within it was born the loving beloved of the Christian faith, the holy champion kind to his own and cruel to his enemies; and when she was created, her mind was filled so with living virtue that, in the mother, she made her a prophet.
sentences.paradiso-12-s7: After the nuptial rites were completed at the sacred font between him and Faith, where they endowed themselves with mutual salvation, the woman who gave her consent to him saw in sleep the marvelous fruit that was to come from him and from his heirs; and so that it might be as it was in its constitution, therefore a spirit was moved to name it from the possessive by which it was entirely (or wholly) possessed.
sentences.paradiso-12-s8: He was called Dominic; and I speak of him thus as of the farmer whom Christ chose for his garden to help him.
sentences.paradiso-12-s1: As soon as the blessed flame had ceased to utter the last word, the holy mill began to turn; and in its circuit it did not complete a whole revolution before another ring closed it, and motion by motion and canto by canto it gathered; a song that so surpasses our Muses, our (uncertain) serenities in those sweet trumpets, as much as the first splendour that it had poured forth.
sentences.paradiso-12-s2: Just as two parallel and similarly colored arches turn through a tender cloud, when Juno bids her handmaiden — one arising from the inner one and the other from the outer — in the manner of the speech of that wandering woman whom love consumed like mere vapors, and they make the people here forewarned, because of the covenant that God established with Noah concerning the world that shall never again be flooded: so around us the two garlands of those everlasting roses are turned, and thus the outer answered the inner.
sentences.paradiso-12-s3: After the jubilation and the other great festival, so much in singing and so much in flashing did light with light, joyous and gentle, come together at the same point and wish to quiet themselves; just as the eyes, moved by pleasure, must both close and be lifted; from the heart of one of the nine lights a voice arose, which made me seem like the needle to a star as I turned toward its place; and it began: 'The love that makes me fair draws me to speak of the other leader, by whom they so well speak of mine.'
sentences.paradiso-12-s4: It is fitting that, wherever one is, the other should be brought; so that, as he unites them in one military force, thus their glory shines together.
